q: Which term means the amount a patient must pay before an insurance plan begins paying covered expenses?
exp: A deductible is the amount the insured person pays out of pocket before the plan begins paying for covered services.
tip: Deductible comes first; coinsurance usually comes after.
optionExplanations: Copayment is not the best answer. The correct choice is Deductible. A deductible is the amount the insured person pays out of pocket before the plan begins paying for covered services.
A deductible is the amount the insured person pays out of pocket before the plan begins paying for covered services.
Premium is not the best answer. The correct choice is Deductible. A deductible is the amount the insured person pays out of pocket before the plan begins paying for covered services.
Coinsurance is not the best answer. The correct choice is Deductible. A deductible is the amount the insured person pays out of pocket before the plan begins paying for covered services.

q: A patient’s plan requires a $25 payment at each office visit. This payment is called a:
exp: A copayment is a fixed amount the patient pays for a covered service, often at the time of the visit.
tip: Co-pay = a set payment.
optionExplanations: A copayment is a fixed amount the patient pays for a covered service, often at the time of the visit.
Premium is not the best answer. The correct choice is Copayment. A copayment is a fixed amount the patient pays for a covered service, often at the time of the visit.
Adjustment is not the best answer. The correct choice is Copayment. A copayment is a fixed amount the patient pays for a covered service, often at the time of the visit.
Deductible is not the best answer. The correct choice is Copayment. A copayment is a fixed amount the patient pays for a covered service, often at the time of the visit.

q: After the deductible is met, a plan pays 80% of the allowed amount. The patient is responsible for the remaining:
exp: Coinsurance is the percentage of the allowed amount shared by the patient after the deductible is applied.
tip: If insurance pays 80%, the patient’s coinsurance is 20%.
optionExplanations: Coinsurance is the percentage of the allowed amount shared by the patient after the deductible is applied.
80% coinsurance is not the best answer. The correct choice is 20% coinsurance. Coinsurance is the percentage of the allowed amount shared by the patient after the deductible is applied.
20% premium is not the best answer. The correct choice is 20% coinsurance. Coinsurance is the percentage of the allowed amount shared by the patient after the deductible is applied.
80% deductible is not the best answer. The correct choice is 20% coinsurance. Coinsurance is the percentage of the allowed amount shared by the patient after the deductible is applied.

q: On an EOB, the amount the insurer recognizes as payable for a covered service is the:
